author: Dr. Jennifer Adams
content:
TELEPHONE ENCOUNTER — 2/5/2026, 4:22 PM

Patient: Robert Morrison (MRN: MRN-78432156)
DOB: 3/15/1952 (Age 73)
Callback number verified: (555) 482-7136

REASON FOR CALL:
Patient called requesting prescription refills for metformin and gabapentin. Additionally requesting review of recent laboratory results from 1/28/2026 blood draw.

CLINICAL DISCUSSION:
Spoke with patient directly. He reports feeling generally stable over the past two weeks since his last PCP visit on 1/27/2026. He states his blood sugars have been running 130-170 fasting and 160-210 post-prandially on his home glucometer, which he checks twice daily per our instruction. He denies any episodes of hypoglycemia, dizziness, or near-syncope. His wife Patricia confirms he has been compliant with his insulin glargine 24 units at bedtime and his sliding scale lispro with meals.

Regarding his neuropathy, he reports the burning and tingling in both feet remains at a 4/10, which is his baseline on gabapentin 300mg TID. He has not missed any doses. He mentions some difficulty sleeping but attributes this to nocturia (getting up 2-3 times per night, consistent with his BPH). He denies any worsening of his lower extremity edema and states his daily weights have been stable at 97-97.5 kg over the past week.

He asks specifically about his kidney function and A1c. I reviewed his labs from 1/28/2026:
- BMP: Cr 1.96 (baseline ~1.95), eGFR 35.4, K 4.8, Na 135, HCO3 23, BUN 38, Glucose 162
- A1c: 7.4% (stable from 7.4% in January)
- CBC: Hgb 11.1, WBC 6.2, Plt 198
- BNP: 410 (stable)

I counseled the patient that his kidney function remains stable at CKD Stage 3b with no significant change from prior values. His A1c of 7.4% is at our agreed-upon target given his age, CKD, and hypoglycemia risk. I reinforced the importance of continued adherence to his low-sodium diet (<2g/day) and his 1.5-2L fluid restriction, especially given his heart failure. I reminded him to avoid NSAIDs and to call if he notices weight gain >3 lbs in a day or worsening shortness of breath or leg swelling.

He also mentions he has attended two of four planned sessions with the social worker for his mood concerns. He says it is "helpful to talk to someone" but remains reluctant to consider medication. PHQ-2 not formally administered by phone but patient denies feeling hopeless or having thoughts of self-harm. He reports his interest in activities is "a little better" and he watched his granddaughter's basketball game last weekend.

MEDICATION REFILLS:
1. Metformin 250mg BID — 90-day supply sent to Walgreens on Main St
2. Gabapentin 300mg TID — 90-day supply sent to Walgreens on Main St

I confirmed both prescriptions were transmitted electronically and advised him to allow 24-48 hours for processing.

PLAN:
- Continue all current medications without changes
- Continue social work counseling sessions; will reassess PHQ-9 at next in-person visit
- Routine labs to be repeated prior to next scheduled PCP visit
- Follow-up: In-office visit scheduled for 3/10/2026 with Dr. Adams
- Patient instructed to call or go to ED if he develops chest pain, worsening dyspnea, weight gain >3 lbs/day, or any signs of stroke (facial drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty — given his A-fib without anticoagulation)

Call duration: 18 minutes. Patient verbalized understanding of all instructions.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Jennifer Adams
Date: 2/5/2026
